- [ ] Truncation + continuation behaviors verified.

- [ ] JSONL logs + artifact manifests validate.

## Test Plan
- E2E script via shared harness and fixtures.

 {"id":"bd-3jpe","title":"Add differential and goal-oriented options to agent plan","description":"## Task
Add differential and goal-oriented option parsing to `pt agent plan` for API surface harmonization.

**SCOPE LIMITATION**: This bead implements CLI flag parsing only. Full feature implementation is deferred to:
- Differential mode: process_triage-9k8.2 (EPIC: Phase 11 - Differential Analysis)
- Goal-oriented: process_triage-95m.* (EPIC: Phase 13 - Goal-Oriented Optimization)
- Predictions: process_triage-yxt.* (EPIC: Phase 12 - Trajectory Prediction)

## Current Implementation
```
pt agent plan [OPTIONS]
  --session <id>           Resume existing session
  --max-candidates <N>     Maximum candidates
  --min-posterior <f64>    Minimum posterior (alias: --threshold)
  --only <filter>          Filter by recommendation
  --deep                   Force deep scan
  --min-age <seconds>      Minimum process age
```

## New Options (Stub Implementation for v1)

### Differential Mode Flags
```
--since <session-id>       Compare against prior session
--since-time <ts|dur>      Compare against time (e.g., "2h", "2026-01-15T12:00:00Z")
```

### Goal-Oriented Flags
```
--goal "<target>"          Resource recovery goal (format: "free 4GB RAM")
```

### Predictive Mode Flags
```
--include-predictions      Add trajectory analysis to output
```

## Implementation Strategy

### Phase 1 (This Bead): Parse flags, return not-implemented warning
```rust
// Add to AgentPlanArgs
#[arg(long, help = "Compare against prior session (coming in v1.2)")]
since: Option<String>,

#[arg(long, help = "Compare against time (coming in v1.2)")]
since_time: Option<String>,

#[arg(long, help = "Resource recovery goal (coming in v1.2)")]
goal: Option<String>,

#[arg(long, help = "Add trajectory analysis (coming in v1.2)")]
include_predictions: bool,
```

### Behavior for v1 (Stub)
When any of these flags are used, the plan is generated normally BUT includes a warning:

```json
{
  "session_id": "pt-20260122-...",
  "candidates": [...],
  "stub_flags": {
    "warning": "feature_not_implemented",
    "message": "Some flags are parsed but not yet functional",
    "flags_used": ["--since"],
    "flags_ignored": ["--since"],
    "workaround": "Use pt agent diff for manual comparison",
    "planned_release": "v1.2"
  }
}
```

**Key behaviors:**
- Exit code: 0 (success with warning, not error)
- Plan is still generated (flags are ignored, not blocking)
- Warning appears in both JSON and summary formats
- Agents can discover the API surface via --help
- Scripts using these flags degrade gracefully

### Phase 2 (Future Beads): Full implementation
- Differential: Depends on session comparison infrastructure
- Goal-oriented: Depends on optimization engine
- Predictions: Depends on trajectory modeling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--since`, `--since-time`, `--goal`, `--include-predictions` flags parse correctly
- [ ] Using these flags returns normal plan WITH warning, not error
- [ ] Help text documents flags with "(coming in v1.2)" notes
- [ ] Exit code 0 even when using stub flags
- [ ] Plan output unchanged when flags not used
- [ ] Warning in both JSON and summary formats
- [ ] `stub_flags` section clearly lists which flags were used and ignored

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_plan_parses_since_flag() {
    // --since value is captured
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_parses_goal_flag() {
    // --goal "free 4GB RAM" is captured
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_stub_flags_produces_warning() {
    // Using --since results in stub_flags warning in output
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_stub_flags_still_generates_plan() {
    // Plan candidates still present when stub flags used
}

#[test]
fn test_plan_no_warning_without_stub_flags() {
    // Normal plan without stub flags has no stub_flags section
}
```

### E2E Test Script (test/e2e_plan_future_flags.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for plan command future flags (stub implementation)

set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_plan_future_flags_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== E2E Test: plan future flags (stubs) ==="

# Test --since flag parsing
log "Testing --since flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --since "fake-session" --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

log "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"

# Verify exit code is 0 (success with warning)
if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ Exit code 0 (success with warning)"
else
    log "⚠ Exit code $EXIT_CODE (may be acceptable if error is clear)"
fi

# Verify warning is present
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags.warning == \"feature_not_implemented\"" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --since returns not-implemented warning"
    USED=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".stub_flags.flags_used | join(\", \")")
    log "  Flags used: $USED"
elif echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".warning" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Warning present (different structure)"
else
    log "✗ --since should return warning"
    echo "$OUTPUT" | head -20 >> "$LOG"
    exit 1
fi

# Verify plan is still generated
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".session_id or .candidates" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Plan still generated despite stub flag"
else
    log "⚠ Plan might not be generated (check output)"
fi

# Test --goal flag parsing  
log "Testing --goal flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --goal "free 4GB RAM" --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --goal accepted (exit 0)"
else
    log "⚠ --goal exit code $EXIT_CODE"
fi

if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags or .warning" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --goal returns warning"
else
    log "✗ --goal should return warning"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --include-predictions flag
log "Testing --include-predictions flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --include-predictions --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --include-predictions accepted (exit 0)"
else
    log "⚠ --include-predictions exit code $EXIT_CODE"
fi

if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags or .warning" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --include-predictions returns warning"
else
    log "✗ --include-predictions should return warning"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --since-time flag
log "Testing --since-time flag..."
set +e
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --since-time "2h" --format json 2>&1)
EXIT_CODE=$?
set -e

if [ "$EXIT_CODE" -eq 0 ]; then
    log "✓ --since-time accepted (exit 0)"
else
    log "⚠ --since-time exit code $EXIT_CODE"
fi

# Test normal plan has no warning
log "Testing normal plan (no stub flags)..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent plan --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".stub_flags" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "⚠ Normal plan has stub_flags (should not)"
else
    log "✓ Normal plan has no stub_flags section"
fi

# Test help mentions stub flags
log "Testing help output..."
HELP=$(pt-core agent plan --help 2>&1)
if echo "$HELP" | grep -q "since"; then
    log "✓ Help mentions --since"
else
    log "✗ Help should mention --since"

- Capture **realistic evidence fixtures** from live runs (CPU/IO/TTY/orphan/parent/cgroup/net), store as versioned test data.

 {"id":"bd-4943","title":"Add missing options to agent snapshot command","description":"## Task
Add missing options to `pt agent snapshot` command per CLI specification.

## Current Implementation
```
pt agent snapshot [OPTIONS]
  --label <LABEL>    Label for the snapshot
```

## Missing Options (per CLI_SPECIFICATION.md:491-506)
```
--top <N>            Limit processes per category (default: 10)
--include-env        Include environment variable summaries
--include-network    Include network connection information
```

## Implementation

### AgentSnapshotArgs struct changes
```rust
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
struct AgentSnapshotArgs {
    /// Label for the snapshot
    #[arg(long)]
    label: Option<String>,
    
    /// Limit to top N processes by resource usage (CPU+memory)
    #[arg(long)]
    top: Option<usize>,
    
    /// Include environment variable summaries (redacted)
    #[arg(long)]
    include_env: bool,
    
    /// Include network connection information (requires lsof/ss)
    #[arg(long)]
    include_network: bool,
}
```

### --top Behavior
Limit resource_hogs categories to top N entries:
```rust
let top_limit = args.top.unwrap_or(10);
resource_hogs.top_cpu.truncate(top_limit);
resource_hogs.top_memory.truncate(top_limit);
resource_hogs.top_io.truncate(top_limit);
```

### --include-env Behavior
Add environment section with REDACTED variable values:
```rust
if args.include_env {
    let env_summary = collect_env_summary(&processes);
    // Values are hashed/redacted, only var names preserved
    // Common vars: PATH, HOME, USER, SHELL, etc.
}
```

Output:
```json
{
  "environment": {
    "summary": "42 unique env vars across 12 processes",
    "common_vars": ["PATH", "HOME", "USER", "SHELL", "TERM"],
    "notable": [
      {"var": "DEBUG", "process_count": 5},
      {"var": "NODE_ENV", "process_count": 3, "values_redacted": true}
    ]
  }
}
```

### --include-network Behavior

#### Tool Precedence
Network information collection follows this precedence:
1. **`ss` (preferred)**: Faster, included in modern Linux (iproute2)
2. **`lsof`**: Fallback, more portable but slower
3. **`/proc/net`**: Last resort, manual parsing

```rust
fn collect_network_info() -> NetworkInfo {
    // Try ss first (fastest, most reliable on Linux)
    if let Ok(info) = collect_via_ss() {
        return NetworkInfo { source: "ss", ..info };
    }
    
    // Fallback to lsof
    if let Ok(info) = collect_via_lsof() {
        return NetworkInfo { source: "lsof", ..info };
    }
    
    // Last resort: /proc/net parsing
    if let Ok(info) = collect_via_proc_net() {
        return NetworkInfo { source: "proc_net", ..info };
    }
    
    // No tools available
    NetworkInfo {
        available: false,
        source: "none",
        error: Some("Network tools unavailable (tried: ss, lsof, /proc/net)".into()),
        ..Default::default()
    }
}
```

#### Output when tools available:
```json
{
  "network": {
    "available": true,
    "source": "ss",
    "listening_ports": [
      {"port": 3000, "proto": "tcp", "pid": 1234, "comm": "node"},
      {"port": 5432, "proto": "tcp", "pid": 567, "comm": "postgres"}
    ],
    "established_connections": [
      {"local": "127.0.0.1:3000", "remote": "127.0.0.1:45678", "pid": 1234, "state": "ESTABLISHED"}
    ],
    "stats": {
      "total_listening": 5,
      "total_established": 23,
      "total_time_wait": 12
    }
  }
}
```

#### Output when tools unavailable:
```json
{
  "network": {
    "available": false,
    "source": "none",
    "error": "Network tools unavailable (tried: ss, lsof, /proc/net)",
    "install_hint": "Install iproute2 for ss or lsof: apt install iproute2 lsof"
  }
}
```

- [ ] `--top` without value uses default (10)
- [ ] `--include-env` adds environment section with redacted values

- [ ] Default behavior unchanged (top=10 implicit, no env/network)

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for agent snapshot options

set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_snapshot_options_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "Testing basic snapshot..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".session_id" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Basic snapshot works"
else
    log "✗ Basic snapshot failed"
    echo "$OUTPUT" >> "$LOG"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --top option
log "Testing --top option..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --top 3 --format json 2>&1)
TOP_CPU_COUNT=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq ".resource_hogs.top_cpu | length // 0" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$TOP_CPU_COUNT" -le 3 ]; then
    log "✓ --top 3 limits output (got $TOP_CPU_COUNT entries)"
else
    log "✗ --top 3 should limit to 3, got $TOP_CPU_COUNT"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --include-env option
log "Testing --include-env option..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --include-env --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".environment" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --include-env adds environment section"
    
    # Verify structure
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".environment.summary" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "✓ Environment has summary field"
    fi
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".environment.common_vars" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        log "✓ Environment has common_vars field"
    fi
else
    log "✗ --include-env should add environment section"
    exit 1
fi

# Test --include-network option
log "Testing --include-network option..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent snapshot --include-network --format json 2>&1)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".network" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ --include-network adds network section"
    
    # Check if tools were available
    AVAILABLE=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".network.available // false")
    SOURCE=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".network.source // \"unknown\"")
    
    if [ "$AVAILABLE" = "true" ]; then
        log "✓ Network info available via $SOURCE"
        
        LISTENING=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq ".network.stats.total_listening // 0")
        log "  Found $LISTENING listening ports"
    else

        if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".network.install_hint" > /dev/null 2>&1; then

        fi
    fi

 {"id":"bd-kwif","title":"Add missing options to agent verify command","description":"## Task
Add missing options to `pt agent verify` command per CLI specification.

## Current Implementation
```
pt agent verify --session <id>
```

## Missing Options (per CLI_SPECIFICATION.md:372-384)
```
--wait <seconds>     Wait for outcomes to stabilize before checking
--check-respawn      Check for respawned processes (supervised restarts)
```

## Implementation

### AgentVerifyArgs struct changes
```rust
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
struct AgentVerifyArgs {
    /// Session ID (required)
    #[arg(long)]
    session: String,
    
    /// Wait for process termination with timeout (seconds, default: 0 = no wait)
    #[arg(long, default_value = "0")]
    wait: u64,
    
    /// Check for respawned processes after kill
    #[arg(long)]
    check_respawn: bool,
}
```

### --wait Behavior
1. Collect PIDs of processes marked for termination in plan
2. Sleep in 250ms intervals, checking /proc/<pid> existence
3. Stop when all PIDs gone OR timeout reached
4. Then proceed with normal verification

```rust
if args.wait > 0 {
    let wait_duration = Duration::from_secs(args.wait);
    let start = Instant::now();
    let target_pids: Vec<u32> = plan.candidates
        .iter()
        .filter(|c| c.recommended_action == "terminate" || c.recommended_action == "kill")
        .map(|c| c.pid)
        .collect();
    
    while start.elapsed() < wait_duration {
        let still_running: Vec<_> = target_pids.iter()
            .filter(|pid| Path::new(&format!("/proc/{}", pid)).exists())
            .collect();
        
        if still_running.is_empty() {
            break;
        }
        thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(250));
    }
}
```

### --check-respawn Behavior

#### Respawn Detection Algorithm
Respawn detection uses **normalized command signatures** to avoid false positives:

```rust
/// Normalize command for respawn comparison
fn normalize_command(cmd: &str) -> String {
    // Extract base command (first word)
    let parts: Vec<&str> = cmd.split_whitespace().collect();
    let base = parts.first().map(|s| {
        // Remove path prefix
        s.rsplit(/).next().unwrap_or(s)
    }).unwrap_or("");
    
    // Include key arguments that identify the process
    // Exclude ephemeral arguments (port numbers, temp files, timestamps)
    let key_args: Vec<&str> = parts.iter().skip(1)
        .filter(|arg| {
            // Keep: test files, watch flags, config files
            !arg.starts_with("--port") &&
            !arg.contains("/tmp/") &&
            !arg.parse::<u32>().is_ok()  // Skip bare numbers
        })
        .take(3)  // Limit to first 3 significant args
        .copied()
        .collect();
    
    format!("{} {}", base, key_args.join(" ")).trim().to_string()
}

// Match respawned processes
if args.check_respawn {
    let killed_signatures: Vec<String> = plan.candidates
        .iter()
        .filter(|c| c.action_taken == Some("terminated".into()))
        .map(|c| normalize_command(&c.cmd_full))
        .collect();
    
    let current_processes = scan_processes();
    
    let respawned: Vec<_> = current_processes
        .iter()
        .filter(|p| {
            let sig = normalize_command(&p.cmd);
            killed_signatures.contains(&sig) && 
            p.start_time > plan.apply_time  // Started after we killed
        })
        .collect();
}
```

#### Supervisor Detection
For respawned processes, attempt to identify the supervisor:
```rust
fn identify_supervisor(ppid: u32) -> Option<String> {
    let parent_cmd = read_proc_comm(ppid)?;
    match parent_cmd.as_str() {
        "systemd" => Some("systemd".into()),
        "supervisord" => Some("supervisord".into()),
        "pm2" => Some("pm2".into()),
        "nodemon" => Some("nodemon".into()),
        "docker-init" => Some("docker".into()),
        _ => None
    }
}
```

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for agent verify --wait and --check-respawn options

set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_verify_options_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== E2E Test: verify options ==="

# Create a test session with some candidates

SESSION=$(pt-core agent plan --format json | jq -r .session_id)
log "Session: $SESSION"

# Test --wait flag timing
log "Testing --wait flag (timing)..."
START=$(date +%s%N)
pt-core agent verify --session "$SESSION" --wait 2 --format json > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
END=$(date +%s%N)
ELAPSED_MS=$(( (END - START) / 1000000 ))

if [ "$ELAPSED_MS" -ge 100 ]; then
    log "✓ --wait caused some delay (elapsed: ${ELAPSED_MS}ms)"
else
    log "✓ --wait exited early (no targets to wait for, elapsed: ${ELAPSED_MS}ms)"

# Test --check-respawn flag structure
log "Testing --check-respawn flag..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent verify --session "$SESSION" --check-respawn --format json 2>&1 || true)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".respawn_check.enabled == true" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ respawn_check.enabled is true"
    
    # Check for expected fields
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".respawn_check.respawned_count != null" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        COUNT=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".respawn_check.respawned_count")
        log "✓ respawned_count field present (value: $COUNT)"
    fi

    log "✗ respawn_check section missing or enabled != true"
    echo "$OUTPUT" >> "$LOG"
    exit 1

    log "✓ Combined flags work"
else
    log "✗ Combined flags failed"
    exit 1

if [ "$ELAPSED" -le 2 ]; then
    log "✓ Short --wait respected (${ELAPSED}s)"

    log "⚠ --wait took longer than expected (${ELAPSED}s)"

 {"id":"bd-v5ba","title":"EPIC: Agent API Harmonization","description":"## Overview
Harmonize the agent/robot CLI interface to ensure coherence between specification, implementation, and planned features. This epic tracks all work needed to align the API surface and improve agent ergonomics.

## Background
A comprehensive review of the agent CLI revealed several inconsistencies:
1. Commands in spec but not implemented (show, status, export aliases)
2. Options in spec but missing from implementation
3. Naming inconsistencies (--threshold vs --min-posterior)
4. Redundant commands that should be consolidated

## Goals
1. **No Lost Features**: All existing and planned functionality preserved
2. **Coherent API**: Consistent naming, behavior, and output formats
3. **Agent Ergonomics**: Intuitive command surface for AI agents
4. **Spec Alignment**: Implementation matches specification
5. **Comprehensive Testing**: Unit, integration, and E2E tests for all changes

## Scope

### Command Surface Harmonization
- [x] Consolidate show/status into sessions command (bd-20wt)
- [x] Add agent export as alias for bundle create (bd-3clf)

### Missing Options Implementation
- [x] verify: Add --wait, --check-respawn (bd-kwif)
- [x] snapshot: Add --top, --include-env, --include-network (bd-4943)
- [x] capabilities: Add --check-action (bd-2o2b)
- [x] diff: Add --focus, --before/--after aliases (bd-2c0w)
- [x] plan: Add --since, --goal, --include-predictions (bd-3jpe) - STUB IMPLEMENTATION

### Naming Unification
- [x] Add --min-posterior as alias for --threshold in plan (bd-2yz0)

### Documentation Updates
- [ ] Update CLI_SPECIFICATION.md with consolidation decisions
- [ ] Update agent-cli-contract.md with harmonized schemas
- [ ] Update AGENTS.md with correct command references

## Child Beads Summary

| Bead | Task | Priority | Tests |
|------|------|----------|-------|
| bd-3clf | agent export alias | P2 | Unit + E2E |
| bd-20wt | sessions consolidation | P2 | Unit + E2E |
| bd-kwif | verify --wait/--check-respawn | P2 | Unit + E2E |
| bd-4943 | snapshot --top/--include-env/--include-network | P2 | Unit + E2E |
| bd-2o2b | capabilities --check-action | P2 | Unit + E2E |
| bd-2c0w | diff --focus + aliases | P2 | Unit + E2E |
| bd-3jpe | plan differential/goal (STUB) | P2 | Unit + E2E |
| bd-2yz0 | --min-posterior alias | P2 | Unit + E2E |

## Testing Requirements (MANDATORY)

### Every Child Bead Must Include:

#### 1. Unit Tests (Rust)
- Flag parsing validation
- Default value verification
- Alias behavior tests
- Error handling tests

#### 2. Integration Tests (Rust)
- End-to-end command execution
- Output format validation
- State changes verified

#### 3. E2E Test Scripts (Bash)
Location: `test/e2e_<feature>.sh`
Format:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_<feature>_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== E2E Test: <feature> ==="
# ... test cases with clear pass/fail logging
log "=== E2E Test PASSED ==="
```

### Test Execution
```bash
# Run all unit tests
cargo test --package pt-core

# Run E2E tests
./test/e2e_runner.sh

# Run specific E2E test
./test/e2e_<feature>.sh
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All commands in spec are implemented or explicitly documented as consolidated
- [ ] All options in spec are implemented (or stubbed with clear "not yet implemented")
- [ ] Naming is consistent across commands
- [ ] Documentation reflects actual implementation
- [ ] No regression in existing functionality
- [ ] All child beads completed
- [ ] **All unit tests pass**
- [ ] **All integration tests pass**
- [ ] **All E2E tests pass with detailed logs**

## Non-Goals
- New major features (watch, fleet) - tracked in separate epics
- Performance optimizations
- New output formats
- Full implementation of differential/goal-oriented modes (stub only for API surface)

## Implementation Notes

### Stub Features (bd-3jpe)
The following flags are parsed but return "not yet implemented" warnings:
- `--since <session>` - Differential analysis
- `--since-time <ts>` - Temporal differential
- `--goal "<target>"` - Goal-oriented optimization
- `--include-predictions` - Trajectory analysis

These allow agents to discover the API surface while full implementation is deferred to:
- process_triage-9k8.2 (Differential mode)
- process_triage-95m.* (Goal-oriented)
- process_triage-yxt.* (Predictions)
